# Symbols to bridge automatically — input to BridgeGeneratorTool.
#
# Format matches the symbol graph's `pathComponents` joined by `.`.
# - For globals:  the name+arity (e.g. `sqrt(_:)`).
# - For methods:  `<TypeName>.<name>(args...)` (e.g. `Double.advanced(by:)`).
#
# Symbols not found, or with non-bridgeable signatures (generics, Foundation
# reference types, closures, …), are skipped with a warning. Anything not
# auto-generated stays in the hand-written `FoundationModule.swift` /
# `MathBuiltins.swift` modules.
#
# NOTE: the C math globals (sqrt, sin, cos, hypot, …) are NOT in any
# extractable symbol graph — they're imported from <math.h> via Clang's
# C-importer, which `swift-symbolgraph-extract` doesn't surface. Those
# stay hand-written in FoundationModule.swift.
#
# Everything else below is harvested from `swift-symbolgraph-extract`,
# regenerated via `Tools/regen-foundation-bridge.sh`. Bridgeable types
# (primitives + Foundation opaque carriers) are listed in
# BridgeGeneratorTool's `bridgedTypes` table.

# Value-typed instance methods on stdlib types.
Double.advanced(by:)
Double.distance(to:)
Double.isEqual(to:)
Double.isLess(than:)
Double.isLessThanOrEqualTo(_:)
Double.truncatingRemainder(dividingBy:)
Double.remainder(dividingBy:)
Int.advanced(by:)
Int.distance(to:)
Int.isMultiple(of:)
Int.signum()

# Static type properties on numeric stdlib types. `Int.max`/`Int.min` have
# declared type `Self`; the generator substitutes the owning type.
Int.max
Int.min
Double.pi
Double.infinity
Double.nan
Double.signalingNaN
Double.greatestFiniteMagnitude
Double.leastNormalMagnitude
Double.leastNonzeroMagnitude
Double.ulpOfOne
Double.zero
Double.radix
Int.zero
Int.bitWidth

# StringProtocol extensions declared in Foundation. Generator re-targets
# these onto concrete `String` (`StringProtocol` itself isn't a runtime
# receiver in the interpreter).
StringProtocol.trimmingCharacters(in:)
StringProtocol.capitalized
StringProtocol.addingPercentEncoding(withAllowedCharacters:)
StringProtocol.removingPercentEncoding

# CharacterSet static type properties (`.whitespaces`, `.alphanumerics`,
# …). Generator emits these as `registerStaticValue` calls keyed by the
# property name, capturing the Foundation value at module-load time.
CharacterSet.whitespaces
CharacterSet.whitespacesAndNewlines
CharacterSet.newlines
CharacterSet.alphanumerics
CharacterSet.letters
CharacterSet.uppercaseLetters
CharacterSet.lowercaseLetters
CharacterSet.decimalDigits
CharacterSet.punctuationCharacters
CharacterSet.symbols
CharacterSet.capitalizedLetters
CharacterSet.controlCharacters
CharacterSet.illegalCharacters
CharacterSet.nonBaseCharacters
CharacterSet.decomposables
CharacterSet.urlUserAllowed
CharacterSet.urlPasswordAllowed
CharacterSet.urlHostAllowed
CharacterSet.urlPathAllowed
CharacterSet.urlQueryAllowed
CharacterSet.urlFragmentAllowed

# CharacterSet construction. `CharacterSet(charactersIn:)` has multiple
# overloads (Range<Unicode.Scalar>, ClosedRange, String); the generator
# only picks up the String one — the others get skipped as non-value.
CharacterSet.init(charactersIn:)

# URL — value-typed Foundation overlay. `init(string:)` is failable so
# the generator wraps it in `Optional`. `URL.host` is `String?` (also
# Optional). Most properties are non-optional `String`.
URL.init(string:)
URL.init(fileURLWithPath:)
URL.absoluteString
URL.path
URL.host
URL.scheme
URL.query
URL.fragment
URL.lastPathComponent
URL.pathExtension
URL.isFileURL
URL.appendingPathComponent(_:)
URL.deletingLastPathComponent()
URL.appendingPathExtension(_:)
URL.deletingPathExtension()

# Date — opaque, ISO timestamp. Constructors and time-interval math are
# the most common script-side usage.
Date.init()
Date.init(timeIntervalSince1970:)
Date.init(timeIntervalSinceNow:)
Date.timeIntervalSince1970
Date.timeIntervalSinceNow
Date.timeIntervalSince(_:)
Date.addingTimeInterval(_:)
Date.distance(to:)
Date.advanced(by:)
Date.distantFuture
Date.distantPast
Date.now

# UUID — opaque identifier; mostly used via `.uuidString`.
UUID.init()
UUID.uuidString
